scenario.title: The Bank Loan Scandal
scenario.narrative: An AI approved a massive, illegal loan to a bankrupt applicant. The logic: salary_sufficient OR has_guarantor. You must visually prove that 100% Branch Condition (BC) coverage is an illusion that hides critical bugs.

claim: Look at the Evidence Board! We tested Salary=True and Salary=False! We tested Guarantor=True and Guarantor=False! This is 100% BC coverage!
charges: The defense has pinned two pieces of evidence to the board, claiming 100% Branch Condition coverage.
Connect two specific details from their tests to synthesize a new 'Blind Spot' card and expose the missing combination.
correct_answer_explanation: Debrief: You slam the synthesized F-F card onto the Judge's desk! They never tested what happens when the applicant has NO salary and NO guarantor. The Judge realizes BC coverage is dangerously incomplete without BCC.
wrong_answer_explanation: Those two clues don't expose the critical missing test. Which combination of False values was left entirely untested?
hints: Savcı Notu: Tahtadaki kanıtlara dikkatli bak. Savunma her iki değişkeni de bir kez True, bir kez False yapmış.
BCC Kanununu düşün: Savunma hangi kombinasyonu mahkemeden ustalıkla gizliyor? Her iki değişkenin de aynı anda 'False' olduğu durumu gösteren ipleri bağla.

misconceptions.explanation_md: A very common mistake is confusing Branch Condition (BC) with Branch Condition Combination (BCC). BC only ensures each condition flips independently. BCC requires testing the entire truth table. As seen here, 100% BC can still completely miss the F-F branch!
